title: crepis neglecta subsp. corymbosa
intro: Crepis neglecta subsp. corymbosa, also known as Hawk's Beard or Yellow Hawk's Beard, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive problems, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Crepis neglecta subsp. corymbosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various ways to harness its health benefits.
